executiveSummary: Iteration 000035 is fully implemented across backend, frontend, and MCP layers. All five user stories and all eight functional requirements are satisfied. The LLM generates YouTube-optimised title and description during orchestration; model credits are loaded from a YAML file and appended to the description; queue entries carry the metadata to the frontend; a preview/edit modal allows the user to review and correct the content before publishing; and the publish_to_youtube MCP tool correctly defaults to the LLM-generated metadata with explicit-argument override and filename fallback. Test coverage (backend unit tests, frontend component tests, and MCP server tests) is comprehensive. No critical gaps were detected.
minorObservations: FR-2 states the YAML should define 'a footer' field, but model_credits.yaml contains only a models list (name, role, optional url). The footer text ('Models used: ...') is composed programmatically by build_credits_block(). The spirit of the requirement is satisfied, but a dedicated footer: key in the YAML (for a static sign-off line) was not added. Low risk.

US-001-AC01 and US-001-AC02 acceptance criteria texts are truncated in the PRD JSON (strings end mid-sentence). The implementation satisfies the full intent inferred from the other ACs and the field definitions, but the truncation makes future audits harder. Recommend fixing the PRD source.

US-004-AC05 ('Visually verified in browser') is a manual verification criterion and cannot be confirmed by code audit alone. No automated visual regression test is present. Recommend adding a Playwright/Cypress smoke test for the modal open/pre-fill/publish flow.

The youtube-publish-dialog component uses a semi-transparent backdrop (bg-black/60) but does not trap focus within the modal (no focus-trap library or manual tabIndex management observed). Screen-reader users relying on keyboard navigation could tab outside the modal, which is an accessibility gap (WCAG 2.1 SC 2.1.2).

Description textarea in the dialog has no character counter, despite the backend enforcing a max_length of 5000 chars. A user who pastes a very long description would not be warned until the upload fails. Minor UX improvement opportunity.
conclusionsAndRecommendations: The iteration is production-ready. All acceptance criteria are met and the implementation is well-tested. Recommended follow-up actions (non-blocking): (1) Add focus-trap to the YouTube publish dialog for WCAG 2.1 compliance. (2) Add a character counter to the description textarea. (3) Add an automated end-to-end (Playwright) test for the publish dialog flow to cover US-004-AC05. (4) Optionally add a footer: key to model_credits.yaml to make the plain-text sign-off line configurable without touching Python code, aligning the YAML structure more precisely with FR-2's wording. (5) Fix the truncated AC texts in the PRD JSON for US-001-AC01 and US-001-AC02.
